title: Non-negotiables for traveling — call mom + dad rule
html:
Non-negotiables for traveling — call mom + dad rule

Source: 2024-06-04 Apple Note. Two-line travel rule written ~3 weeks into the May–Aug 2024 Asia trip — first in-vault articulation of the call-parents minimum that becomes a stable rule across the six month adventure.

TL;DR

  • Two non-negotiables, both family: call mom at least once (else text), call dad at least once (else text). No business, no social, no fitness — just the parents.
  • Pairs with the existing non negotiables for traveling concept page; this is the Apple-note origin of that concept.
  • Written from somewhere in early-trip Asia (June 4 = ~Vietnam/Ha Giang timeframe, before Shanghai semester starts in late August).
  • Empty third bullet ("-") suggests he stopped before adding more — possibly because the parents-rule was the only thing that needed writing down.
  • A high-signal debt of gratitude moment: even mid-adventure, the unstated baseline is don't lose touch with the people who made the adventure possible.

Pattern

The two-rule structure (mom + dad, call > text) is the same minimalism as the soul as beacon manifestos — only the irreducible thing gets written.

Open questions

  • Did he actually keep this rule for the full six months? (Worth densify-checking against later China summaries.)
  • Was there a specific incident that prompted writing this down — a missed call?

Related

  • shanghai study abroad
  • six month adventure creed
  • debt of gratitude

  • ha giang loop
